title: This “Roasted Exoplanet” Has a Wild Orbit
gist: HD 80606 b — a hot Jupiter four times Jupiter's mass discovered by UCL students in 2009 — has the most eccentric, comet-like orbit of any known planet, swinging from ten times closer than Mercury to nearly Earth's distance from its star over a 111-day revolution.

insights.claim: HD 80606 b, a gas giant four times Jupiter's mass located 270 light-years away, has the most eccentric orbit of any known planet — a comet-like flattened oval.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Extreme exoplanets stress-test planetary-formation models; basic astronomy with no market read.
insights.quote: had the most eccentric orbit of any other planet ever discovered, a flattened oval that looked more like the orbit of a comet

insights.claim: At closest approach HD 80606 b veers ten times closer to its star than Mercury before zooming nearly as far as Earth's distance over its 111-day revolution, earning it NASA's 'roasted exoplanet' label as a hot Jupiter.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: An extreme natural laboratory for tidal heating and atmospheric physics.
insights.quote: At its closest approach, HD 80606 b veers ten times closer to its star than Mercury is to the sun
insights.locator: At its closest approach, HD 80606 b
